title: Market Brief — Wednesday, June 3, 2026
summary: GPU availability is loosening hard across every class. H100 remains tightest at 16% util-avail (Ornn), but even that eased 13pp in 30 days. A100 sits at 34%, while H200 and B200 have cracked wide open at 46% and 43% respectively, bordering oversupply after historic 35-40pp utilization drops. H100 spot fell 4.5% day-over-day to $2.67/hr, the sharpest single-day move in the stack.
paragraphs: GPU availability is loosening hard across every class. H100 remains tightest at 16% util-avail (Ornn), but even that eased 13pp in 30 days. A100 sits at 34%, while H200 and B200 have cracked wide open at 46% and 43% respectively, bordering oversupply after historic 35-40pp utilization drops. H100 spot fell 4.5% day-over-day to $2.67/hr, the sharpest single-day move in the stack.

At TCO-adjusted costs (1.25x spot), Llama 70B inference runs $6.33/M on A100, $9.75/M on H100, $9.45/M on H200, and $5.70/M on B200. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output is selling at a loss of roughly $5.36/M on B200 hardware, burning cash to hold share. OpenAI's gpt-oss-120b at $0.18/M output is even deeper underwater. Grades: OpenAI Burning Cash (oss tier), Anthropic Strong (Opus/Sonnet margins hold), Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 at $6.24/hr spot with 43% availability and falling utilization is overvalued relative to rapidly softening demand — short B200 capacity commitments. A100 at $1.00/hr median is undervalued for cost-efficient Llama-class inference at $5.06/M production cost with positive margins above medium tier — accumulate A100 spot reservations now.
bullets: 16% util-avail on H100 makes it the only GPU class still near the scarcity threshold.
46% util-avail on H200 reflects a 40pp collapse in utilization over 30 days, the steepest drop across all classes.
204% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the most unstable compute contract trading today.
$0.18/M output on OpenAI gpt-oss-120b sits roughly $5.52/M below TCO-adjusted A100 production cost.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier output tokens cost 78% less than GPT-4 launch pricing.
$80/M output on o3 Pro is 235x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M for comparable-class models.
